There was an error while loading. [Please reload this page](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/181743). # {{title}} Quote reply edited ### Uh oh! There was an error while loading. [Please reload this page](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/181743). # {{editor}}'s edit {{actor}} deleted this content . # {{editor}}'s edit ## [![](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/96466166?s=64&v=4)\ seshasandeep](https://github.com/seshasandeep) [on Dec 12, 2025Dec 12, 2025](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/181743\#discussion-9240554) - | | | --- | | We've identified a couple of issues regarding organization management within our Enterprise account that we'd like to bring to your attention.
Currently, Enterprise owners can navigate to Enterprise > Organizations to view a list of organizations associated with our Enterprise. However, there's no feature to filter this list. Specifically, we are unable to filter for active organizations (i.e., non-archived organizations). This makes it challenging to quickly identify and manage our currently operational organizations.
Furthermore, we've observed an inconsistency in the organization list order when comparing the GitHub web UI and the mobile application; both display different order of organizations. This discrepancy is problematic and leads to confusion.
We also attempted to sort the organizations by adding a "zzzz-" prefix to an Organization's display name, but this did not result in an alphabetical sort, and we don't want to update the organization name slug to sort the list.
We believe that the ability to filter organizations and ensure consistent display across platforms are fundamental features for effective Enterprise management.
Could you please investigate these issues and provide a solution?
As Enterprise owners it is important for us to have this feature to navigate freely between categories of organizations.
Please consider this as a priority feature request, rather than a general feedback.
Thank you, | BetaWas this translation helpful? - Explore our [Product Roadmap](https://github.com/orgs/github/projects/4247), which details upcoming major releases and initiatives.
**What you can do in the meantime 💻**
- Upvote and comment on other user feedback Discussions that resonate with you.
- Add more information at any point! Useful details include: use cases, relevant labels, desired outcomes, and any accompanying screenshots.
As a member of the GitHub community, your participation is essential. While we can't promise that every suggestion will be implemented, we want to emphasize that your feedback is instrumental in guiding our decisions and priorities.
Thank you once again for your contribution to making GitHub even better! We're grateful for your ongoing support and collaboration in shaping the future of our platform. ⭐ | BetaWas this translation helpful? web.title: Viewing people's roles in an organization - GitHub Docs
web.description: To filter the list by role, select the Role dropdown menu and click a role. Screenshot of the list of organization members. In the header of the list, a. If ...
web.markdown:
[Skip to main content](https://docs.github.com/en/account-and-profile/how-tos/organization-membership/viewing-peoples-roles-in-an-organization#main-content)

# Viewing people's roles in an organization

You can view a list of the people in your organization and filter by their role.

## Who can use this feature?

Organization members

Copy as Markdown

1. In the upper-right corner of GitHub, click your profile picture, then click **Organizations**.

2. Click the name of your organization.

3. Under your organization name, click **People**.



![Screenshot of the horizontal navigation bar for an organization. A tab, labeled with a person icon and "People," is outlined in dark orange.](https://docs.github.com/assets/cb-18976/images/help/organizations/organization-people-tab.png)

4. To filter the list by role, select the **Role** dropdown menu and click a role.



![Screenshot of the list of organization members. In the header of the list, a dropdown menu, labeled "Role," is outlined in dark orange.](https://docs.github.com/assets/cb-34919/images/help/organizations/view-list-of-people-in-org-by-role.png)


If your organization uses GitHub Enterprise Cloud, you can also view the enterprise owners who manage billing settings and policies for all your enterprise's organizations. For more information, see [the GitHub Enterprise Cloud documentation](https://docs.github.com/en/enterprise-cloud@latest/account-and-profile/setting-up-and-managing-your-personal-account-on-github/managing-your-membership-in-organizations/viewing-peoples-roles-in-an-organization#view-enterprise-owners-and-their-roles-in-an-organization).

## [Next steps](https://docs.github.com/en/account-and-profile/how-tos/organization-membership/viewing-peoples-roles-in-an-organization\#next-steps)

To learn more about organization roles, see [Roles in an organization](https://docs.github.com/en/organizations/managing-peoples-access-to-your-organization-with-roles/roles-in-an-organization).

web.title: Filtering repositories in your organization using the repository table
web.description: Filtering the repository table with the advanced filter builder. In the upper-right corner of GitHub, click your profile picture, then click Organizations.
web.markdown:
[Skip to main content](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/how-tos/secure-at-scale/configure-organization-security/manage-your-coverage/filtering-repositories-in-your-organization-using-the-repository-table#main-content)

# Filtering repositories in your organization using the repository table

You can filter the repository table for your organization to better manage the security settings of specific repositories.

## Who can use this feature?

Organization owners, security managers, and organization members with the **admin** role

Copy as Markdown

## In this article

You can filter the repository table in your organization to quickly find and manage specific repositories when applying security configurations or managing Advanced Security license usage in the organization. This article explains how to use filters in the repository table.

## [Filtering the repository table with the search bar](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/how-tos/secure-at-scale/configure-organization-security/manage-your-coverage/filtering-repositories-in-your-organization-using-the-repository-table\#filtering-the-repository-table-with-the-search-bar)

1. In the upper-right corner of GitHub, click your profile picture, then click **Organizations**.

2. Under your organization name, click **Settings**. If you cannot see the "Settings" tab, select the  dropdown menu, then click **Settings**.



![Screenshot of the tabs in an organization's profile. The "Settings" tab is outlined in dark orange.](https://docs.github.com/assets/cb-49309/images/help/discussions/org-settings-global-nav-update.png)

3. In the "Security" section of the sidebar, select the **Advanced Security** dropdown menu, then click **Repositories**.

4. In the "Apply configurations" section, use the **Search repositories** search bar to filter repositories in your organization in one of two ways:
   - Find repositories by name with free-text search
   - Select filters from the dropdown menu that appears once you click the search bar

## [Filtering the repository table with the advanced filter builder](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/how-tos/secure-at-scale/configure-organization-security/manage-your-coverage/filtering-repositories-in-your-organization-using-the-repository-table\#filtering-the-repository-table-with-the-advanced-filter-builder)

1. In the upper-right corner of GitHub, click your profile picture, then click **Organizations**.

2. Under your organization name, click **Settings**. If you cannot see the "Settings" tab, select the  dropdown menu, then click **Settings**.



![Screenshot of the tabs in an organization's profile. The "Settings" tab is outlined in dark orange.](https://docs.github.com/assets/cb-49309/images/help/discussions/org-settings-global-nav-update.png)

3. In the "Security" section of the sidebar, select the **Advanced Security** dropdown menu, then click **Repositories**.

4. To open the advanced filter dialog, in the "Apply configurations" section, click **Filter**.

5. In the "Advanced filters" window, select the **Add a filter** dropdown menu, then click a filter.

6. To search for repositories matching the selected filter, fill out the available fields for that filter, then click **Apply**. You can repeat this process to add as many filters as you would like to your search.

7. Optionally, to remove a filter from your search, click **Filter**. In the row of the filter you want to remove, click , then click **Apply**.

### Body
I have been exploring my GitHub profile and noticed the list of organizations, but I'm curious about the criteria or algorithm that determines their display order.
Is the order based on factors like the date of joining, activity level, or is it customizable by the user?

[Reload](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/164013) to refresh your session.Dismiss alert {{ message }} # [![@community](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/93784371?s=60&v=4)\ GitHub Community](https://github.com/community) # \[FEATURE REQUEST\] Organization Profile - Sort `People` by contributions vs age \#164013 Unanswered [hoivikaj](https://github.com/hoivikaj) asked this question in [Enterprise](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/categories/enterprise) [\[FEATURE REQUEST\] Organization Profile - Sort \`People\` by contributions vs age](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/164013#top)#164013 [![@hoivikaj](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/38190936?s=40&v=4)\\ hoivikaj](https://github.com/hoivikaj) on Jun 24, 2025Jun 25, 2025· 4 comments [Return to top](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/164013#top) Discussion options ### Uh oh! There was an error while loading. [Please reload this page](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/164013). # {{title}} Quote reply ## [![](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/38190936?s=64&v=4)\ hoivikaj](https://github.com/hoivikaj) [on Jun 24, 2025Jun 25, 2025](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/164013\#discussion-8491302) - | | | --- | | ### Select Topic Area
Product Feedback
### Body
On organization profiles, the `People` section displays avatars for the first 20 accounts to join the organization.
Proposal: Have this list of people instead be users sorted by # of commits (or some other metric).
In large organizations, especially ones like mine that are using SCIM for provisioning, there was really no logic to the order in which users were onboarded into the organization. Most of the users featured in our `people` section rarely use GitHub (and don't even have custom avatars set), where users that interact every day are not shown.
Screenshot of section I am referring to using the github.com/github org.
